title: RFE: Provide a streaming image search API
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

The current search API searches in parallel with a wait group, which means that an application has to wait on until all search requests too complete before being able to show results to the user. This also has the benefit of not needing to wait on the slowest registry.

If the API could provide a streaming option, the search results could in theory appear quicker for the user, the downside would be that ordering, limiting, error handling would be more complicated and podman would need special handling for this use case.

The search registries I have specified are shown below, searches take around ~ 1 second too complete so it's too bad.
```
unqualified-search-registries = ["registry.fedoraproject.org", "registry.access.redhat.com", "docker.io", "quay.io"]
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @jelly!

Search is parallelized and waiting ~1 second seems acceptable to me. Certainly, this may be limited by the registries and a bit by the network (although it's not much data) but a `podman images` may take longer if there are many images.  Note that search is not a reliable way to check for the presence of images. The semantics of the endpoints on registries are not standardized.

It would be very low on my priority list but PRs are always welcome :)  Before opening a PR, please discuss how the data would be presented to the user.

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I am going to close the issue as not planned.  Two years later and we have no progress even on how the data should be represented in the streams, so I consider it to be not important.

Feel free to reopen if I am mistaken, and there is still a desire for that feature.
